title: Flowers at the Market
summary: A child visits a colourful flower stall with her mother at an outdoor market. She discovers different flowers and helps choose a bunch to take home.
body: My name is Lily, and today I went to the outdoor market with my mum. The market was busy and full of colour. We stopped at a flower stall near the entrance. The stall had big buckets of flowers everywhere. I could see red roses, yellow sunflowers, and white daisies. The smell was very sweet and lovely. The flower seller was a kind old woman with a bright smile. She let me touch the soft petals of a pink flower. Mum asked for a bunch of mixed flowers. I helped choose the ones I liked best. The seller wrapped them in paper and tied a ribbon around them. Mum paid five pounds for the flowers. I carried them all the way home. When we got back, Mum put the flowers in a vase with water. Our kitchen looked beautiful with the bright flowers on the table.

annotations.exampleEn: We buy fresh vegetables at the outdoor market every weekend.
The flower stall was decorated with many colourful bouquets.
The flowers stood upright in large buckets of water.
She picked a daisy from the garden and put it in her hair.
Sunflowers always turn to face the light.
The soft petals fell onto the ground in the wind.
He gave his mother a bunch of roses for her birthday.
The seller wrapped the flowers in brown paper.
She tied a red ribbon around the gift box.
Put the flowers in a vase so they stay fresh longer.
We met our friends near the entrance to the market.
The teacher greeted every student with a bright smile.

grammarNotes.title: Simple Past Tense
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Chúng ta dùng thì quá khứ đơn để kể về những việc đã xảy ra và kết thúc trong quá khứ. Ví dụ: "We stopped at a flower stall" (Chúng tôi đã dừng lại ở gian hàng hoa). Động từ thường thêm '-ed': stop → stopped, look → looked.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: We use the simple past tense to describe actions that happened and finished in the past. Regular verbs add '-ed': stop → stopped. Irregular verbs change form: go → went, see → saw, put → put.

grammarNotes.title: Prepositions of Place: at, in, near
grammarNotes.bodyVi: "At" chỉ vị trí cụ thể (at the market, at the stall). "Near" nghĩa là gần (near the entrance). "In" dùng khi ở bên trong không gian (in a vase, in a bucket). Học cách dùng các giới từ này giúp mô tả vị trí rõ ràng hơn.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'At' points to a specific location (at the market). 'Near' means close to something (near the entrance). 'In' is used when something is inside a container or space (in a vase). These prepositions help describe where things are.

translationVi: Tên tôi là Lily, và hôm nay tôi đi chợ ngoài trời cùng với mẹ. Khu chợ rất đông đúc và rực rỡ màu sắc. Chúng tôi dừng lại ở một gian hàng hoa gần cổng vào. Gian hàng có những chiếc xô hoa lớn ở khắp nơi. Tôi có thể thấy hoa hồng đỏ, hoa hướng dương vàng, và hoa cúc trắng. Mùi hương rất ngọt ngào và thơm tho. Người bán hoa là một bà cụ tốt bụng với nụ cười rạng rỡ. Bà để tôi sờ vào những cánh hoa mềm mại của một bông hoa màu hồng. Mẹ hỏi mua một bó hoa hỗn hợp. Tôi giúp chọn những bông hoa mình thích nhất. Người bán gói chúng lại bằng giấy và buộc một dây ruy băng xung quanh. Mẹ trả năm bảng Anh cho bó hoa. Tôi mang chúng về nhà suốt chặng đường. Khi về đến nơi, mẹ cắm hoa vào một chiếc bình có nước. Căn bếp của chúng tôi trông thật đẹp với những bông hoa tươi sáng trên bàn.

questions.prompt: Who did Lily go to the market with?
questions.explanation: The story says 'today I went to the outdoor market with my mum.'
questions.hint: Think about who Lily's family member was.

options: At the back of the market
questions.explanation: The story says 'We stopped at a flower stall near the entrance.'
questions.hint: Where did they stop first when they arrived?

questions.prompt: Which of these flowers did Lily NOT see at the stall?
questions.explanation: The story mentions red roses, yellow sunflowers, and white daisies — not purple lavender.
questions.hint: Read the list of flowers in the story carefully.

questions.prompt: How did the flower stall smell?
questions.explanation: The story says 'The smell was very sweet and lovely.'
questions.hint: What word did the story use to describe the smell?

questions.prompt: What did the flower seller look like?
options: A young man with a hat
A kind old woman with a bright smile
A young woman with curly hair
questions.explanation: The story describes her as 'a kind old woman with a bright smile.'
questions.hint: Think about the seller's age and personality.

questions.prompt: What did the seller let Lily do?
questions.explanation: The story says 'She let me touch the soft petals of a pink flower.'
questions.hint: What did Lily do with her hands at the stall?

questions.prompt: How much did the bunch of flowers cost?
questions.explanation: The story says 'Mum paid five pounds for the flowers.'
questions.hint: Look for the price mentioned in the story.

questions.explanation: The story says 'I carried them all the way home.'
questions.hint: Who is the narrator of this story?

questions.prompt: Where did Mum put the flowers when they got home?
questions.explanation: The story says 'Mum put the flowers in a vase with water.'
questions.hint: What do you usually put flowers in to keep them fresh?

questions.prompt: Which room looked beautiful after they got home?
questions.explanation: The story says 'Our kitchen looked beautiful with the bright flowers on the table.'
questions.hint: Which room had the table where the flowers were placed?

questions.prompt: What colour was the flower whose petals Lily touched?
questions.explanation: The story says 'She let me touch the soft petals of a pink flower.'
questions.hint: What colour was specifically mentioned for the flower she touched?

cloze.template: My name is Lily, and today I went to the outdoor market with my mum. The market was busy and full of {{b1}}. We stopped at a flower {{b2}} near the entrance. The stall had big {{b3}} of flowers everywhere. I could see red roses, yellow sunflowers, and white daisies. The smell was very sweet and {{b4}}. The flower seller was a kind old woman with a bright {{b5}}. She let me touch the soft {{b6}} of a pink flower. Mum asked for a {{b7}} of mixed flowers. I helped choose the ones I liked best. The seller {{b8}} them in paper and tied a ribbon around them. Mum paid five pounds for the flowers. I {{b9}} them all the way home. When we got back, Mum put the flowers in a {{b10}} with water. Our kitchen looked beautiful with the bright flowers on the table.

blanks.explanation: 'Full of colour' describes the visual variety of the market.
An outdoor market selling point is usually called a 'stall'.
Flowers at markets are typically kept upright in large 'buckets' of water.
The story describes the smell as 'very sweet and lovely'.
The seller had 'a bright smile' — it showed she was friendly.
'Petals' are the soft, coloured parts of a flower.
A group of flowers held together is called a 'bunch'.
'Wrapped in paper' means the flowers were covered with paper for carrying.
'I carried them' means I held and transported them the whole way.
Flowers are kept in a 'vase' — a tall container designed for this purpose.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Lily's mum bought flowers just to make the kitchen look beautiful. Do you think buying something beautiful — even if it's not necessary — is a good use of money? Why or why not?
